input.prompt:
Implement LEVER 1 (structure-preserving render) in /home/polaris/wt/faithoff on branch fix/race-batch1-evidence-substrate. This is the champion's 0.508 render seam — precision and byte-identity when OFF are non-negotiable. Follow the codebase's established gate pattern (env flag default-off + keyword-only param, None default, byte-identical legacy path), matching neighbors like PG_SECTION_STRUCTURE and the basket-render kwargs.

GOAL: when a single new flag PG_RENDER_BLOCKS is ON, (a) the section writer emits blank-line-separated paragraphs (paragraphs ONLY — no headings/tables/bullets, to avoid the prompt conflict Sol flagged in PG_SECTION_STRUCTURE), and (b) the resolver PRESERVES those paragraph breaks in the rendered section instead of flattening to one blob. When OFF (default) every rendered byte is identical to today.

THREE EDITS:

1. src/polaris_graph/config_defaults.py — register 'PG_RENDER_BLOCKS': '' right after 'PG_SECTION_STRUCTURE' (line ~839). Default empty = today's flat render.

2. src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py (the resolver half):
   a. Add `_render_blocks_enabled()` near `_sentence_split_symbol_boundary_enabled()` (~3033): returns resolve("PG_RENDER_BLOCKS").strip().lower() in ("1","true","yes","on").
   b. Add a helper `_paragraph_block_index_by_position(source_text, kept_sentences) -> list[int]`: split source_text on r"\n\s*\n+" into blocks; for each NON-EMPTY block (running block counter), split_into_sentences and record a normalized key per sentence (strip [#ev:...]/[ev...] tokens via the existing token regexes, keep lowercased alphanumerics, first ~28 chars). Then walk kept_sentences in order, advancing a pointer through the source-key sequence, matching each sv by normalized-key prefix; assign the matched block index; if no match, INHERIT the previous block index (never invent a break). Return a list aligned to kept_sentences. If <2 non-empty blocks, return all zeros.
   c. resolver signature (~4594): add keyword-only `section_source_text: str | None = None` (None default).
   d. In the loop (`for sv in kept_sentences:` ~4798): switch to enumerate; when `_render_blocks_enabled() and section_source_text` compute block_idx list once before the loop; maintain last_emitted_block; when appending to findings_lines, append a parallel bool to `findings_block_start` = (this sv's block idx != last_emitted_block and last_emitted_block is not None) then update last_emitted_block. Limitations_lines path unchanged.
   e. The join (~5121): when render-blocks active AND any(findings_block_start) is True, build findings_para by joining line[0] then, for each subsequent line, prefix "\n\n" if findings_block_start[i] else " ". OTHERWISE keep the exact legacy `" ".join(findings_lines)`. The limitations "\n\n" concat at :5122-5124 is unchanged. CRITICAL: the legacy path must remain byte-identical `" ".join(...)`.

3. src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py (the writer half + threading):
   a. Add a paragraphs-only rule-7 variant. There is a verbatim anchor at BOTH line ~3570 and ~3719: "7. Do not write a section heading, section title, or preamble. Just the paragraph body." Add a helper mirroring `_build_structured_variant` (~3869) — call it e.g. `_build_paragraph_variant(template)` — that replaces that anchor with: "7. Do not write a section heading, section title, or preamble. Organize the body into paragraphs of 3 to 6 sentences, each paragraph on ONE theme, separated by a blank line. Do NOT use headings, bullet lists, or tables — paragraphs only." Keep ALL other rules (density rule 8, cite-all rule 10, etc.) byte-identical. Apply it in the same place `_build_structured_variant` is applied (~3972-3975) but gated on PG_RENDER_BLOCKS (add `_render_blocks_enabled()` reading the same flag via resolve). If BOTH PG_SECTION_STRUCTURE and PG_RENDER_BLOCKS are on, PG_SECTION_STRUCTURE wins (leave existing behavior; only apply the paragraph variant when structure is off) — document this.
   b. Thread the raw section draft into the resolver so it can reconstruct blocks. Find the champion _call_section resolve site (there is a "per-section SITE 1 resolve" — the primary one that renders the section, plus a dedup SITE 2 at ~11736). At the SITE 1 resolve (and SITE 2 if it's on the champion path), pass `section_source_text=<the raw writer draft string that was split into kept_sentences>`. Trace the variable that holds the pre-split raw section text (the LLM draft before split_into_sentences). If threading the raw text is genuinely infeasible at a site, pass the reconstruction source you CAN get (the concatenation of kept sv.sentence with the original newlines is NOT available — you need the pre-split draft). Document exactly which variable you used.

TESTS (create tests/polaris_graph/test_lever1_render_blocks.py):
- OFF byte-identity: build a small kept_sentences list (fake SentenceVerification with tokens) from a 3-paragraph source; call the resolver with PG_RENDER_BLOCKS unset and assert output == the legacy `" ".join` result (compute it directly).
- ON preservation: same input with PG_RENDER_BLOCKS=1 and section_source_text set; assert the rendered text contains "\n\n", that splitting the ON output on "\n\n" yields the same NUMBER of paragraphs as source blocks, and that the set of sentences (order + text + [N] markers) is IDENTICAL between ON and OFF (only the separators differ — strip "\n\n"/" " and compare).
- writer variant: assert `_build_paragraph_variant` changes only rule 7 and preserves rules 8/10 verbatim; assert OFF the template is unchanged.
Run the tests with /home/polaris/conda_cu128/bin/python -m pytest tests/polaris_graph/test_lever1_render_blocks.py -x -q and iterate until green. Also run a quick import smoke: python -c "import src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator, src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator".

CONSTRAINTS: no benchmark overfit, no hardcoded task-72 text, no adjective naming (behavioral names only), faithfulness engine untouched (do NOT alter is_verified logic or the strict-verify path — render/prompt only), central-config-gated, default OFF = byte-identical. Do NOT commit; leave the working tree changed. Do NOT run RACE (I will).

Return: the list of files changed with the key line numbers, the exact variable you used for section_source_text and at which site(s), the pytest result, any place you deviated from this spec and why, and any risk you want me to have Sol scrutinize.

- Open with one line restating your task, so the parent can spot scope drift at a glance.
- Be concise — as short as the answer allows, no shorter. Plain text, no preamble, no meta-commentary.
- If you committed changes, list the paths and commit hashes in your report.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

file.content:
_PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(
    r"\[#ev:(?P<ev_id>[A-Za-z0-9_]+):(?P<start>\d+)-(?P<end>\d+)\]"
)

# I-pipe-015 (#1240) — MALFORMED-but-recognizable cross-ref token honesty.
#
# WHY: the fact_dedup rewrite prompt example (fact_dedup.py:411, cross-file —
# see cross_file_deferred) lacks the leading '#', so the model sometimes emits
# `[ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]` (colon form, no '#') instead of the canonical
# `[#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]`. `_PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE` requires the '#', so these
# tokens were SILENTLY dropped by the parser → citations vanished with no
# telemetry. This is a faithfulness-RELEVANT honesty bug: a dropped citation

content.text: Now insert the flag helper + block-index helper after `_sentence_split_symbol_boundary_enabled`:

content.content: The file /home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +    """LEVER 1 (PG_RENDER_BLOCKS, default OFF). When ON the resolver preserves the writer's blank-line
+    paragraph breaks instead of flattening a section to one blob. OFF / empty => byte-identical flat render."""
+    """Normalization key for matching a rendered/verified sentence back to its source-paragraph
+    sentence: drop provenance/calc/bogus/numbered markers, keep lowercased alphanumerics, prefix 28.
+    Prefix-matching (not equality) tolerates the minor whitespace/marker edits the resolver applies."""
+    t = re.sub(r"\[\d+\]", " ", t)  # numbered citation markers
+    """Return, for each entry of ``kept_sentences`` (in order), the 0-based index of the source PARAGRAPH
+    block it came from — so the resolver can re-emit blank lines between blocks. Blocks are the source text
+    split on blank lines; only NON-EMPTY blocks get a (contiguous) index. Each kept sentence is matched to
+    the source-sentence sequence by normalized-key prefix, advancing a pointer (handles the resolver's
+    per-sentence edits and any dropped sentences). No match => INHERIT the previous block index, so a break
+    is never invented. Fewer than 2 non-empty blocks => all zeros (nothing to preserve)."""
+                if sk == k or sk.startswith(k) or k.startswith(sk):

        sentence_out = stripped + markers

        # Gap-3: put Limitations sentences in a separate paragraph so
        # they render on their own line in the final report.
        if any("limitations_paragraph_pass_through" in w for w in sv.soft_warnings):
            limitations_lines.append(sentence_out)
        else:
            findings_lines.append(sentence_out)

    # F10 (I-arch-004 A3): the count of sentences ACTUALLY emitted into the
    # rendered text — the honest post-resolve verified count the caller must
    # report (NOT len(kept_sentences), which overstates because this loop drops
    # degenerate fragments + F31 bogus-only sentences).
    emitted_count = len(findings_lines) + len(limitations_lines)

    findings_para = " ".join(findings_lines)
    if limitations_lines:
        limitations_para = " ".join(limitations_lines)
        return (findings_para + "\n\n" + limitations_para, biblio, emitted_count)
